title: OT Simulator Network Atom
description: Validates a network atom YAML file in design/networks/. A network atom describes a single network segment as an atomic building block for OT environment composition. Ethernet networks require a subnet property; serial networks must not have one.

schema_version.description: Schema version for network atom YAML files. Increment when breaking changes are made to the network atom structure.

network.description: Network segment identity and classification metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for this network segment, used to reference the network in environment definitions and placement network assignments (e.g., 'wt-level1', 'mfg-serial-bus', 'ps-wan-link').

name.description: Human-readable name for this network segment, suitable for display in topology diagrams and documentation.

type.description: Network type determining the communication medium and addressing model. ethernet: IP-addressable Ethernet segment (may be physical copper, fiber, or logical WAN/cellular). serial-rs485: EIA-485 multidrop serial bus (no IP addressing; devices invisible to network scanners). serial-rs232: EIA-232 point-to-point serial link (no IP addressing; single device connection).

description.description: Human-readable description of this network segment's purpose, era, and OT security characteristics.

properties.description: Physical and capability properties of this network segment. Ethernet segments require a subnet. Serial segments must not have a subnet (serial buses have no IP addressing). VLAN is only meaningful when managed_switch is true.

subnet.description: IP subnet for this Ethernet segment in CIDR notation (e.g., '10.10.10.0/24'). Required for all ethernet-type networks. Must not be present on serial-type networks (serial buses have no IP addressing and are invisible to network scanners).

vlan.description: IEEE 802.1Q VLAN ID (1-4094) assigned to this segment. Only applicable when managed_switch is true. Omit for unmanaged switches (which cannot configure VLANs) and serial networks.

managed_switch.description: Whether this network segment uses a managed switch. Managed switches support SPAN port mirroring (enabling passive IDS/ICS monitoring tools), VLAN configuration, and port-level access control. Unmanaged switches provide no visibility into traffic -- a critical OT monitoring blind spot.

span_capable.description: Whether this segment supports SPAN (Switch Port Analyzer) port mirroring for passive traffic capture. True only when managed_switch is true. Passive monitoring tools (Dragos, Zeek, Cisco CyberVision) require SPAN capability to observe segment traffic without altering it.
